title: Click Save to integrate PagerDuty with Atlas.

sections.heading: How it Works
sections.body:
With a PagerDuty integration, you can send Atlas cluster event data
to PagerDuty when Atlas alerts that you specify are triggered.
PagerDuty can create a new incident for the corresponding
service, filter additional alerts from the same source into that
incident, and alert on-call PagerDuty users.

Once the Atlas alert has been resolved, PagerDuty resolves the
incident.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To create, update, or remove a PagerDuty integration, you must have
[Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access to the project. Users with
[Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access must add themselves to the project
as a `Project Owner`.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
To integrate Atlas with PagerDuty, you must have a PagerDuty
account.

If you do not have an existing PagerDuty account, you can sign up at
[https://www.pagerduty.com/sign-up/](https://www.pagerduty.com/sign-up/).

All new PagerDuty keys use their [Events API v2](https://developer.pagerduty.com/docs/ZG9jOjExMDI5NTgw-events-api-v2-overview).

If you have an Events API v1 key, you can continue to use
that key with Atlas.

sections.heading: Support
sections.body:
If you need help with your Atlas PagerDuty integration, contact
MongoDB Support.

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
**Atlascli:**Atlas CLITo create or update a PagerDuty integration using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas integrations create PAGER_DUTY [options]

```To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas integrations create PAGER\_DUTY](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-integrations-create-PAGER_DUTY).**Ui:**Atlas UITo integrate with PagerDuty using the Atlas UI, navigate to
the PagerDuty integration card:Click Configure for the PagerDuty integration card.
You can configure PagerDuty with either your PagerDuty credentials
or your PagerDuty API (Application Programming Interface) key.You can configure an Atlas integration with PagerDuty in two ways:* By signing into PagerDuty from the Atlas
  **Project Integrations** page, or

* By providing a valid **Integration Key**.

sections.heading: Sign into PagerDuty
sections.body:
You are redirected to PagerDuty.

From the dropdown menu, select the PagerDuty service that you
want to integrate with MongoDB.

> **Note:** You can select multiple services, but you will be prompted
> to pick a single default in the next step.

PagerDuty redirects you back to your Atlas project and displays a
PagerDuty Configuration modal.

From the PagerDuty Configuration modal, select one
default service to integrate. Atlas automatically
provides an integration key.

When you click Test Integration, the PagerDuty
configuration modal displays a banner with a success or
failure message.

sections.heading: Provide an Integration Key
sections.body:
When you click Test Integration, the PagerDuty
configuration modal displays a banner with a success or
failure message.

> **Important:** If the **Integration Key** that you specify is invalid,
> the PagerDuty Events API doesn't fail and the **Test
> Integration** button might falsely indicate a successful
> integration. To confirm that your integration is successful,
> Atlas sends an initial alert. Check your PagerDuty
> dashboard for an initial incident created by that Atlas
> alert.

sections.heading: Remove a PagerDuty Integration
sections.body:
**Atlascli:**Atlas CLITo delete a third-party integration using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas integrations delete <integrationType> [options]

```To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas integrations delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-integrations-delete).**Ui:**Atlas UITo remove the PagerDuty integration from your Atlas project using the Atlas UI, click **Remove** on the PagerDuty
card in your Atlas project's **Project Integrations**.
